title: isMutable: false on Metaplex NFT metadata permanently locks it — a broken URI or trait can never be fixed

params.passDetail: isMutable: true — metadata stays updatable, so a broken URI or trait can be corrected later.
params.failDetail: isMutable: false permanently freezes the NFT/collection metadata on-chain: the update authority can never fix a broken metadata URI, wrong trait, or dead image link. The mint succeeds and the happy path runs — the asset is just silently locked forever.
params.absentCallDetail: No NFT mint / metadata-create call found in this scope.
params.absentArgDetail: isMutable not set explicitly; the SDK default applies (mutable), so no forbidden literal is present.

provenance.note: Seen in solana-developers/compressed-nfts (scripts/createAndMint.ts:112 & :145, scripts/verboseCreateAndMint.ts:202 & :254): both the collection metadata and the per-NFT MetadataArgs are minted with isMutable: false. The mint compiles and succeeds, but freezes the metadata permanently — a broken/placeholder URI (the demo ships notarealurl links) can never be corrected. Agents copy this canonical example verbatim and ship un-fixable NFTs.
